title: support
author: Technical-Writer-SSR
body: How does Vrite handle real-time collaboration between multiple contributors, and are there any best practices for managing conflicts or simultaneous edits in the documentation

title: Collaboration websocket fails to connect when loading content pieces (self-hosted)
author: lidianzhong
body: Hi, Vrite is a great WYSIWYG editor and I'm trying to deploy it in a self-hosted setup. I’m using Nginx as a reverse proxy and have configured `COOKIE_DOMAIN `as well as the four `PUBLIC_*` URLs. Everything seems to be working except for the collaboration WebSocket. When loading a content piece, the collab WebSocket fails to connect, causing the article to not load. Here is the console output: Below is my docker.env configuration: ``` COOKIE_DOMAIN=hzau.top PUBLIC_COLLAB_URL=https://collab.vrite.hzau.top PUBLIC_APP_URL=https://app.vrite.hzau.top PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api.vrite.hzau.top PUBLIC_ASSETS_URL=https://assets.vrite.hzau.top ``` And here is my Nginx config for the collab service: docker compose up doesn’t show any errors in the logs. If needed, I can provide complete logs as well. Any suggestions on how to fix this WebSocket issue? Thanks!

title: Stand-alone local editor?
author: Pomax
body:
Just ran across  https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=36446045 and I've been looking for something to replace Typora for a while now, but I can't for the life of me find this "standalone app, extracted from the larger Vrite CMS project". I'm 100% not interested in signing up for some cloud service and use a browser to write 100,000 word documents (which ruled out MarkText as alternative, it performs incredibly poorly on actually long markdown documents) but I'm more than happy to donate an appropriate amount if the standalone editor can replace Typora for me.

That does require being able to find it, though =D

title: Feature: Reopen resolved threads
author: janreppien
body:
Sometimes we close a thread and realize later that the issue discussed in it is not resolved. It would be handy to be able to reopen the threads so that the discussion can be complete traced by new people joining. Currently, we open a new thread and if necessary copy old messages by hand.
Overall, the ability to add comments to a section in the editor is really awesome, and speeds up communication immensely.

title: Feature: Notification for accounts mentioned in comment
author: janreppien
body:
It would be nice if accounts mentioned with `@account_name` in comments would get a notification and/or an e-mail. 

Background: We often edited large documents with many people, and some sections need to be checked by at least two of them. Currently, we have to scroll (or search with the browsers search function) through the comments and check if our account name is mentioned somewhere.

As always, I love your product. The ability to edit documents concurrently is absolutely amazing!

title: Unable to add row to table
author: janreppien
body:
Firstly, I think vrite is absolutely amazing, and I am very happy with it. 
During my testing, I encountered only one error. 
Sometimes (I am not able to pinpoint the circumstances leading to the error) it is not possible to add a new row to an existing table. When pressing the button to add a row, the following errors are logged in my browsers console:

![Screenshot 2024-11-29 at 13 35 36](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4193f382-0aeb-44a3-9b31-a4b5ff110916)


The MDX-Export of the minimal example looks like this:

```
---
description: ""
slug: "minimal-example"
title: "Minimal example"
---

| Prüfschritt | Seite       | Fehlerbeschreibung | Lösungsvorschlag |
| ----------- | ----------- | ------------------ | ---------------- |
| Test        | 1           |                    | Fehlerfehler     |
| Test 2      | 1,2         | Fehler             |                  |
| Test 3      | alle Seiten | fehler 2           |                  |
```

Thank you very much for all your work on this amazing tool :)

title: RSS/Atom feed for blog?
author: gBasil

title: Add Dockerfile for Extension Service
author: sFritsch09
body:
There is a **Dockerfile** missing for the Extension Service at `apps/backend/extensions`. 
This Service enhances self-hostable Docker with extensions such as the Dev.to API, operating on Port 7777.

title: GitLab support
author: almereyda
body:
Many technical teams work on GitLab, which is another preferred git forge, next to #38 or GitHub.

This issue is left here to track the case of GitLab support in Vrite separately from the other issue.

Steps to pass could be:

- [ ] GitLab OAuth/OIDC login
- [ ] git-sync provider for GitLab in [`backend/src/lib/git-sync/providers`](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/tree/main/packages/backend/src/lib/git-sync/providers)

Looking forward to test this and provide feedback.

title: Kubernetes Deployment
author: sFritsch09
body: I’m in the process of setting up Vrite on Kubernetes using a Helm Chart, but I’m facing issues with the Api and Assets—they’re not functioning, and I’m unable to retrieve any logs for debugging purposes. Additionally, when I access the vrite api Pod and execute node index.js, there’s no response or output. Has anyone successfully deployed Vrite on Kubernetes and can offer some guidance?

title: Link invalid when testing invitation
author: almereyda
body:
When testing locally, emails and logins work.

But invite links pasted into a private browser window which has a second user already authenticated fail with:

> ## Link invalid
> Workspace invite

![grafik](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/1645308/b22f7f92-44a1-45da-b257-512ed8eb4dc4)

title: Bug: Importing a table from MDX-based source-control renders it as text.
author: JorgeAtPaladin
body:
![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 44 57](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/3e016dfd-8ad4-4331-b01f-426af00e56b3)


If I make a change to an mdx table within github, eg. change some text, and then re-sync the file to vrite by pressing `pull`, the table is no longer loaded as a table.

It may be sensible to store the tables in hypertext within mdx? not sure how to fix this, storing tables as .md tables is always going to be a bit lossfull as well.

**environment**: This table is present within a custom element view.

title: Bug: Setting source control transformer to mdx sets it back to markdown
author: JorgeAtPaladin
body:
When I try to connect my git repository with mdx content, this content can't be loaded:

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 15 33](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/2af676e1-8bcb-4438-9ed1-50902c6247d0)

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 16 30](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/c16d2653-6f70-428a-9b4f-6cb1d63ffd60)


Once I press `save`:

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 16 47](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/c25e848b-ca82-4a30-9132-90e287d104a0)

(Notice a separate bug where the editor view infinitely loads now, not sure why that is)

Pressing `sync now`:

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 17 28](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/c2cb5fb7-ecab-44ae-871f-877d95547381)

Says latest changes synced in the bottom right (already gone) in green and shows this. View of settings by pressing the github logo:

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 17 52](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/e3ce331e-240d-4ce5-87a5-1029656b616d)

Notice the `markdown` transformer... I have the mdx extension, which is apparent as I could select it.

title: Feature: Github source control integration: Ensure limited permissions
author: JorgeAtPaladin
body:
![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 01 49](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/2de0f3dd-849c-46cb-bb38-b347034d25f0)

For now when I connect a github for the source control integration, this is the permission view I get.

Once I press the green `authorize` button, the window closes and the app is authorized as can be seen in my settings:

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 06 11](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/eaf7e5d6-fb75-4955-9595-0e167abfb24b)

It is unclear what `Act on your behalf` means, permissions wise.

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 06 28](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/598d6d81-8254-4bed-bec4-c060ce0ddf18)

But for other applications, they do not need to be globally connected to my account, instead they are just connected to specific repositories.

![Capture d’écran 2024-04-30 à 12 07 23](https://github.com/vriteio/vrite/assets/87263486/14cdf852-1e69-4e6d-9d27-6250460eeb7a)

title: GitOps support?
author: jcrben
body:
I really like this - especially the way the WYSIWG tables work! I haven't found anything that looks this powerful other than Confluence, and it looks like something which might replace how I use Confluence.

I'd like to persist using markdown, using a sort of GitOps approach.

One thing I noticed is that the export looks to be either Markdown or HTML. In the case of these advanced tables, will the markdown table work for the round-trip - will it be able to maintain the complexity of your WSYIWYG tables? Is it possible to export markdown but have the tables embedded as HTML inside the markdown? Once tables get complex and edited via WSYIWYG I've found that HTML is more worthwhile than markdown.

title: Missing app and api in docker-compose
author: subatiq
body:
I'm not sure if it's by design, but `docker-compose.yml` is missing App and API services. Documentation does not mention how to deploy them separately.

Specifically if I follow this page, only MongoDB, MinIO, Redis and Weaviate services are deployed:
https://docs.vrite.io/self-hosting/docker/

title: docs: unclear how to add rich content via api

body: I would like to provide an image via the api to be included in a post but when passed via Markdown it isn't parsed. How are you supposed to add rich content to the page?
